title: Exceptions and guarantees deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Exceptions and guarantees: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? When an exception occurs, automatic objects in exited scopes are abandoned without destructors running.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Stack unwinding destroys fully constructed automatic objects; RAII uses those destructors to release resources on exceptional exits.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Stack unwinding destroys fully constructed automatic objects; RAII uses those destructors to release resources on exceptional exits.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Stack unwinding destroys fully constructed automatic objects; RAII uses those destructors to release resources on exceptional exits.
When an exception occurs, automatic objects in exited scopes are abandoned without destructors running.

answer.explanation: Stack unwinding destroys fully constructed automatic objects; RAII uses those destructors to release resources on exceptional exits.

items.title: Exceptions and guarantees: complete the language rule

items.title: Exceptions and guarantees: executable application
items.prompt: Implement checked_divide that throws std::invalid_argument for a zero divisor, call it inside try, and print either integer quotient or division-by-zero from the catch handler.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: typed exception for a violated operation precondition

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises typed exception for a violated operation precondition under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
